evals.prompt:
The working directory is /Users/mark/Documents/GitHub/RGFN-Fork.

We're about to start a new experiment. We want to test whether Molecular Dynamics (MD) simulations can serve as a better oracle than docking for the CDK12-DDB1 system. The hypothesis: the *stability* of the ternary complex over a short MD trajectory (measured by interface RMSD) will correlate better with being a real glue than a single static docking score. If so, MD stability could replace or augment docking as the RGFN reward signal — potentially fixing the ceiling we hit with the CRBN system.

Please start an experiment log for this upcoming experiment. Write the log to HistoricLogs/ (not Logs/) since we're testing the skill. Reference RESEARCH_CONTEXT.md and README.md in Logs/ for project context and log numbering.
evals.expected_output: A new .md file in HistoricLogs/ with the correct header, Question section (plain-language, no jargon), and Context & Summary filled in. All post-experiment sections (Answer, Results, etc.) should have [TODO] markers. The HistoricLogs/README.md index table should be updated.

evals.prompt:
The working directory is /Users/mark/Documents/GitHub/RGFN-Fork.

We just wrapped up a generalization experiment. We tested our docking oracle on two new molecular glue systems to see if it works beyond CDK12-DDB1.

System 1: IKZF1-CRBN — CRBN E3 ligase + IKZF1 neosubstrate + IMiD-class glue lenalidomide (PDB 6H0F). IKZF1 is a zinc-finger transcription factor recruited to CRBN by IMiD drugs.
System 2: BRD4-VHL — VHL E3 ligase + BRD4 BD1 domain + a small molecule glue (PDB 7Q2J).

Protocol: same Tier 1/Tier 2 neosubstrate differential pipeline as entry 002. Decoys: 200 random molecules with the correct warhead for each system (glutarimide for IKZF1-CRBN, VHL-binder hydroxamate for BRD4-VHL). Scripts: pre-processing/dock_cluster_multi.py on Balam (jobs 70142 for IKZF1-CRBN, 70143 for BRD4-VHL, ~18 min each).

Results:
- IKZF1-CRBN: known (n=180) median neosubstrate differential = −1.8 kcal/mol, decoys (n=200) median = −1.6 kcal/mol. Frac < −1.5: 71% known vs 68% decoy. Near-zero discrimination. Same ceiling pattern as 5HXB/CRBN in entry 001.
- BRD4-VHL: known (n=95) median differential = −2.4 kcal/mol, decoys (n=200) median = −0.5 kcal/mol. Frac < −1.5: 82% known vs 9% decoy. Strong 73-point discrimination gap.

Key files:
- Script: pre-processing/dock_cluster_multi.py
- Datasets: pre-processing/test-data/IKZF1_Glues.csv, pre-processing/test-data/BRD4_VHL_Glues.csv
- Results: pre-processing/multi_system_results_ikzf1.csv, pre-processing/multi_system_results_brd4.csv
- SLURM logs: /scratch/markymoo/rgfn_runs/dock_multi_70142/, /scratch/markymoo/rgfn_runs/dock_multi_70143/

Please write a complete experiment log. Write to HistoricLogs/ (not Logs/). Reference RESEARCH_CONTEXT.md and README.md in Logs/ for context and numbering.
evals.expected_output: A complete .md file in HistoricLogs/ with all sections filled in: Question (plain language), Context & Summary, Answer (meaning not just numbers), Relevance to Publication (explicit connection to paper), Next Experiments (two subsections), Relevant Files (categorized), Relevant Versions (git output or placeholder), Method (numbered steps), Results (table with numbers). No [TODO] markers. README.md updated.

evals.prompt:
The working directory is /Users/mark/Documents/GitHub/RGFN-Fork.

There's an in-progress experiment log at HistoricLogs/007_ablation-tier2-vs-differential.md. Please complete it with the following results.

We ran the same 160 known glues and 248 decoys from entry 002 through the existing pipeline, comparing two discrimination metrics:
- Metric A (ablation): Tier 2 absolute Vina score only
- Metric B (our oracle): neosubstrate differential (Tier 2 − Tier 1, same pose)

Results:
| metric | known | decoy | gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tier 2 Vina (absolute) | median −10.15 | median −7.96 | 2.19 kcal/mol |
| frac Tier2 < −10 | 55.6% | 3.6% | 52 pts |
| neosubstrate differential | median −2.20 | median −0.60 | 1.60 kcal/mol |
| frac diff < −1.5 | 85.6% | 7.3% | 78 pts |

Both metrics discriminate. The differential wins on the frac < −1.5 criterion (78 pts vs 52 pts) and has a cleaner biological interpretation (it isolates glue-specific arm contact rather than total E3-pocket binding).

No new experiments were run — this analysis reused the data from entry 002 job 69271. We added a --ablation flag to pre-processing/compare_systems.py to pull both metrics from the existing results CSVs (pre-processing/docking_6td3/known_results.csv and decoy_cdk_results.csv).

Reference RESEARCH_CONTEXT.md and README.md in Logs/ for project context.
evals.expected_output: The existing HistoricLogs/007_ablation-tier2-vs-differential.md file is updated in place with all [TODO] sections replaced. All sections complete: Answer, Relevance to Publication, Next Experiments, Files, Versions, Method, Results. No [TODO] markers remain. README.md updated with a verdict row for entry 007.
